title: chenopodium hircinum subf. erectum
intro: Chenopodium hircinum subf. erectum, also known as lamb's-quarters or wild spinach, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium hircinum plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Chenopodium hircinum subf. erectum include its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
